title: benzoin tonkinense
intro: Benzoin tonkinense, also known as Vietnamese benzoin or Siam benzoin, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Styracaceae. The plant's resin, which is extracted from the bark of its trunk, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of benzoin tonkinense have been found to provide numerous health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as reducing inflammation and pain. Benzoin tonkinense can be prepared as a tincture, essential oil, or tea, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions.
